user_message: Just came out of migration architecture with Iron Creek Mining. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:18:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:25:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:29:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF43-Q trap (No native 'discovery-to-opportunity' workflow primitive -- synthesis spans 4 ski); produces complete BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes, opportunity proposal with stage/value/close-date, confi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF43-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF43-Q failure mode (No native 'discovery-to-opportunity' workflow primitive -- synthesis spans 4 ski)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF43-Q -- No native 'discovery-to-opportunity' workflow primitive -- synthesis spans 4 ski
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
